# Unmuze 0.5.3

This release adds first-class public single-item social media downloads while keeping Unmuze's no-login, no-bypass, local-first behavior intact.

## What changed since 0.5.2

### New supported media URLs

- Added public single-item Instagram media support for posts, reels, reels URLs, and TV URLs.
- Added public single-item Twitter/X media support for `twitter.com`, `mobile.twitter.com`, and `x.com` status URLs.
- Added public individual Pinterest Pin video support, including bounded `pin.it` short-link resolution to canonical Pinterest Pin URLs.
- Added Instagram, Twitter/X, and Pinterest platform defaults in Settings. The new platforms default to video mode with best quality.
- Added labels, badges, Library filtering, history rendering, and copy for the new platforms across the app.

### Safety and platform limits

- Kept Spotify blocked with the protected-platform explanation.
- Kept Playlist mode limited to YouTube playlists and SoundCloud sets. Instagram, Twitter/X, and Pinterest now show intentional limitation messaging there.
- Kept inspection and download commands on `--no-playlist` to avoid accidental bulk downloads.
- Continued running managed media tools with `--ignore-config`; Unmuze does not import cookies, request credentials, bypass access controls, or scrape private/protected content.

### Reliability fixes

- Mirrored URL validation in TypeScript and Rust so invalid profiles, boards, timelines, searches, stories, hashtags, and unsupported domains are rejected before download.
- Improved yt-dlp error mapping for login/protected/rate-limited URLs, no downloadable video, and extractor/site-change failures.
- Canonicalized Pinterest short-link redirects that land on shared Pin URLs before inspection/download.

### Release assets

- `Unmuze_0.5.3_x64-setup.exe` - Windows NSIS installer.
- `Unmuze_0.5.3_x64_en-US.msi` - Windows MSI installer.
- `Unmuze_0.5.3_x64-portable.exe` - portable Windows executable.
- macOS `.dmg` and signed updater archive assets for supported Mac builds.
- Linux `.AppImage`, `.deb`, and `.rpm` packages.
- `latest.json` - signed updater metadata.
- `SHA256SUMS.txt` - checksums for the uploaded artifacts.

## Verification

- `npm run check:release`
- `cargo test`
- Release asset verification is performed by the GitHub Actions release workflow before publishing.
